description: Confounder: The model assumes the weight remains float32, so multiplication preserves float3

_meta.explanation: The rule 'RMSNorm with elementwise_affine=True and a float32 weight preserves float32 output even when input is bfloat16, because no cast back to input dtype occurs after the weight multiplication.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes the weight remains float32, so multiplication preserves float32. But the weight was explicitly cast to bfloat16, triggering the hidden cast of x to bfloat16 before multiplication.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'RMSNorm with elementwise_affine=True and a float32 weight preserves float32 output even when input is bfloat16, because no cast back to input dtype occurs after the weight multiplication.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
